decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is currently at a moderate level (500 W/mK) but has room to grow within bounds. Increasing conductivity by 40% (within the 2.0x limit) will enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the hotspot.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution away from the chip center, directly lowering the peak temperature violation.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if conductivity becomes too high relative to the base plate extraction capacity
Future iterations may require increasing base_material.conductivity if spreader tuning saturates
